_comment: Config for tools/seal.py. Copy to engram.config.json next to your repo root (or set ENGRAM_CONFIG). All paths are relative to the repo root. No hardcoded folder names live in seal.py — they all come from here.

seal._comment: The private directory to encrypt at rest, and where its ciphertext lands.

tamper._comment: Tamper-evidence snapshot of your governance/structure notes. structure_lock holds a SHA-256 manifest of every file matched below.

append_only._comment: Optional. An append-only inbox where past entries must never be edited or deleted (only new ones appended). Each entry starts with a heading like '### ID-001'. Leave file empty/omit this block to skip.

append_only_files._comment: Generalized append-only tamper-evidence for ARBITRARY record files (dev-spine Session/Issue/Fix indexes, a money ledger, ...). snapshot records a per-row SHA-256; verify allows appending new rows but FAILS (nonzero) on any edit or deletion of a previously-seen row. Omit or empty this list to leave the feature inactive.
row_scope 'block' keys a multi-line record by its heading (heading line -> next heading). Use for issue/fix logs where one id owns several lines.
A money ledger: only 'lock' and 'row_key_regex'/'row_scope' are optional; they fall back to append_only_file_defaults below.

append_only_file_defaults._comment: Defaults applied to any append_only_files entry that omits these keys. row_key_regex group(1) is the stable row id; rows that don't match (headers, separators, prose) are skipped. lock_suffix builds a lock path when an entry omits 'lock'.
